index.name: Nifty 50
index.description: The NIFTY 50 is India's flagship index representing the 50 largest companies on NSE. Covers 13 sectors and ~65% of NSE's free-float market cap. Most widely tracked equity benchmark in India. Uses free-float market capitalization methodology. Base value of 1000 set on November 3, 1995.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: India's largest conglomerate spanning petrochemicals, refining, retail (Reliance Retail — 15,000+ stores) and digital services (Jio with 450M+ subscribers). Mukesh Ambani-led ₹19 lakh crore market cap giant. New Energy investments of ₹75,000 Cr in solar, hydrogen and batteries.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: India's largest private bank serving 85M+ customers via 8,000+ branches. Merged with HDFC Ltd in 2023 creating a financial colossus. Market leader in home loans, auto loans and credit cards. Best-in-class NPA ratios at 1.2%.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: India's second-largest private bank with industry-best ROE of 18%+. Technology-first approach with iMobile Pay app (10M+ users). Strong in retail, corporate and SME lending. CEO Sandeep Bakhshi steering consistent outperformance.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Global IT services giant co-founded by N.R. Narayana Murthy in 1981 with ₹10,000. Cobalt cloud platform, AI-first strategy. Serves Fortune 500 clients in 50+ countries. India's second-largest IT firm with $18B+ annual revenue.

holdings.name: TCS
holdings.description: Tata Consultancy Services — India's largest IT company. First Indian company to cross $200B market cap. Global leader in IT services, consulting and business solutions with $25B+ annual revenue. Serves 1,200+ clients in 150 countries.

holdings.name: Bharti Airtel
holdings.description: India's largest telecom operator by revenue with 380M+ subscribers. Active 5G rollout across 5,000+ Indian cities. Operates across 17 African countries. Strong enterprise and home broadband growth under Airtel Business.

holdings.name: ITC Ltd
holdings.description: Diversified conglomerate with cigarettes (60% profit), FMCG brands (Aashirvaad, Sunfeast, Yippee, Bingo), hotels (ITC Hotels chain), paperboards and agribusiness. Consistent high-dividend payer. Demerging hotels division in 2024.

holdings.name: L&T
holdings.description: Larsen & Toubro — India's largest engineering & construction company. Builds metros, airports, power plants, defense systems and nuclear plants. ₹4 lakh crore order book. Also strong in IT through LTIMindtree and L&T Technology Services.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: India's third-largest private bank with 10,000+ touch points. Acquired Citibank India's retail portfolio in 2023 significantly strengthening wealth management and credit cards. Known for Burgundy private banking and digital transformation.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Premium private bank built by Uday Kotak from a bill discounting NBFC in 1985. Known for conservative lending, best-in-class asset quality and low NPAs. 811 digital banking app serves 35M+ customers.

holdings.name: HCL Technologies
holdings.description: India's third-largest IT company by revenue with $13B+ annual revenue. Products-led IT company with strong HCL Software business. Engineering services and infrastructure management leader. IBM software IP acquisition worth $1.8B.

holdings.name: State Bank of India
holdings.description: India's largest bank commanding 23% deposit share. ₹55 lakh crore in assets, 22,000+ branches and 65,000+ ATMs. YONO digital banking app has 40M+ users. Gateway bank for government schemes and public sector lending.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical
holdings.description: India's largest pharma company by market cap. Dominant in US specialty dermatology (Ilumya for psoriasis, Cequa for dry eyes). Taro Pharmaceutical subsidiary adds branded Rx strength in North America.

holdings.name: Bajaj Finance
holdings.description: India's most valuable NBFC with 85M+ customer franchise. Built the most sophisticated consumer lending franchise — EMI cards at retail stores, FDs, insurance, and digital payments. 25%+ annual growth engine.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: India's largest power generator with 73 GW operational capacity. State-owned utility transitioning aggressively to renewables — targeting 60 GW renewable capacity by 2032. Consistent high-dividend payer with government backing.

holdings.name: Maruti Suzuki
holdings.description: India's largest car manufacturer with 41%+ market share. JV with Suzuki Japan since 1982. Dominates entry and mid-segments with Alto, Swift, Baleno, Dzire, Ertiga. Transitioning to CNG and hybrid platforms with EV coming.

holdings.name: Titan Company
holdings.description: Tata Group's jewellery and watches powerhouse. Tanishq is India's most trusted jewellery brand (₹40,000+ Cr revenue). Fastrack, Titan watches, Sonata. Expanding into eyewear (Titan Eye+) and fragrances.

holdings.name: Wipro
holdings.description: Azim Premji's IT conglomerate transforming under CEO Srinivas Pallia. Strategic European acquisitions (Capco for BFSI consulting, Rizing for SAP). Consumer care division (Santoor, Wipro Baby) is a profitable side business.

holdings.name: UltraTech Cement
holdings.description: India's largest cement manufacturer with 130 MT capacity. Aditya Birla Group flagship. Pan-India presence with 23+ integrated plants, 30+ grinding units. Consolidating via acquisitions — Kesoram, Heidelberg India.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corp
holdings.description: India's monopoly power transmission utility owning 70% of inter-state transmission network. 175,000 circuit km of transmission lines. Regulated 15.5% post-tax returns. Building Green Energy Corridors for renewables integration.

index.name: Nifty Bank
index.description: The Nifty Bank Index tracks 12 most liquid and large-cap Indian banking stocks on NSE. Serves as benchmark for banking ETFs and F&O derivatives. Heavily influenced by RBI monetary policy, credit growth cycles, and NPA trends. One of the most actively traded indices globally — Nifty Bank weekly options are among the world's highest volume derivative contracts.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Post-merger with HDFC Ltd, a financial colossus with ₹25 lakh crore in assets. India's largest private bank by market cap. Processes 1 in 5 retail credit card transactions in India. 8,700+ branches and 21,000+ ATMs.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Aggressive growth under CEO Sandeep Bakhshi. Industry-best ROE of 18%+ among large private banks. iMobile Pay has 10M+ users. Strong in all segments — retail mortgages, corporate, SME and international banking.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Premium banking franchise with exceptional asset quality — GNPA below 2%. Converted from NBFC in 2003. Burgundy Wealth management for HNIs and 811 zero-balance digital account are standout differentiated products.

holdings.name: State Bank of India
holdings.description: India's largest bank with ₹55 lakh crore in assets, 22,000+ branches and 65,000+ ATMs. YONO app has 40M+ registered users. Dominant in MSME, agri and government-backed lending. Consistent dividend payer.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: Third-largest private bank with 10,000+ touch points. Citibank India acquisition in 2023 strengthened wealth management (AUM doubled) and credit cards (now top 3 issuer). Digital-first under CEO Amitabh Chaudhry.

holdings.name: Bank of Baroda
holdings.description: Second-largest PSU bank post Vijaya Bank and Dena Bank merger in 2019. International presence in 100+ countries. Post-NPA cleanup showing sustainable ROE improvement and strong loan growth.

holdings.name: IndusInd Bank
holdings.description: Mid-size private bank with stronghold in vehicle finance (CV and 2W loans accounting for 30% book) and microfinance. Nexxt credit card. Strong corporate banking franchise and growing retail deposit franchise.

holdings.name: Punjab National Bank
holdings.description: India's second-largest PSU bank by branches, founded in 1894 in Lahore. Post-NPA cleanup showing sustainable profitability improvement. Government ownership provides stability. Strong in MSME, agri and priority sector lending.

holdings.name: Federal Bank
holdings.description: Kerala-headquartered private bank with 1,400+ branches. Strong NRI deposit franchise (Kerala diaspora in Gulf). Fintech partnerships (Google Pay, Paytm) drive digital transactions. Consistent performer with improving ROA.

holdings.name: IDFC First Bank
holdings.description: Transformed from an infrastructure NBFC to a full-service retail bank under MD V. Vaidyanathan. Rapid deposit mobilisation, strong microfinance and vehicle finance franchises. Merged with IDFC Ltd creating a clean balance sheet.

index.name: BSE SENSEX
index.description: The S&P BSE SENSEX is India's oldest and most recognized index, calculated since 1986 with base year 1978–79 = 100. Comprises 30 financially sound blue-chip companies listed on BSE — Asia's oldest exchange (est. 1875). Serves as the primary barometer of the Indian equity market and is used globally as India's economic indicator. Crossed 80,000 for the first time in July 2024.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: Mukesh Ambani's conglomerate covering petrochemicals, retail (India's largest with 15,000+ stores), Jio telecom (450M subscribers), and new energy (₹75,000 Cr green energy investment plan by 2030 in solar, hydrogen and batteries).

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Post-HDFC merger, a financial colossus. Largest private bank by market cap. Processing scale: 1 in 5 credit card transactions in India goes through HDFC Bank systems. 8,700+ branches across India.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Technology-first banking approach with 18%+ ROE — best among large Indian private banks. iMobile Pay app boasts 10M+ users. Mortgage, credit cards, and commercial banking growth firing simultaneously.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Founded with $250 in 1981 by N.R. Narayana Murthy, now an $80B+ company. Cobalt cloud platform gaining global traction. AI-first strategy driving consulting-led large deal wins with $4B+ TCV per quarter.

holdings.name: TCS
holdings.description: Tata Sons' crown jewel and India's most valuable IT company. Known for world-class talent development, remarkably low attrition at 12% and highest NPS client satisfaction scores in global IT industry.

holdings.name: Bajaj Finance
holdings.description: India's most valuable NBFC with 85M+ customer franchise. EMI financing at 150,000+ partner merchants, FD platform, insurance distribution. 30%+ loan AUM growth engine with best-in-class credit systems.

holdings.name: Hindustan Unilever
holdings.description: Indian arm of Unilever with 50+ brands across home care (Surf, Rin), personal care (Dove, Lux) and food (Knorr, Horlicks). Present in 9 out of 10 Indian households. High-dividend payer for decades.

holdings.name: Maruti Suzuki
holdings.description: India's largest car maker with 41%+ market share. JV with Suzuki Japan since 1982. Dominates entry and mid-segments (Alto, Swift, Baleno, Dzire, Ertiga). Transitioning to CNG, hybrid and EV platforms.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Premium private bank with exceptional asset quality GNPA below 2%. Burgundy Wealth management for HNIs, 811 digital banking for millennials. Conservative lending philosophy creating superior long-term ROE.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: Third-largest private bank strengthening via Citibank India acquisition. Wealth management AUM doubled. Top 3 credit card issuer. Digital-led branches and strong SME banking franchise across India.

holdings.name: L&T
holdings.description: India's largest EPC company with ₹4 lakh crore order book. Builds metros, airports, power plants, defence systems. LTIMindtree and L&T Technology Services add high-margin IT revenue to the conglomerate.

holdings.name: Asian Paints
holdings.description: India's dominant paints brand with 55%+ market share in decorative paints. Beautiful Homes studios for interior design. Expanded into waterproofing, wood finishes and adhesives. 65-year-old brand with pristine moat.

holdings.name: ITC Ltd
holdings.description: Diversified conglomerate with cigarettes (60% profit), FMCG (Aashirvaad, Sunfeast, Yippee, Bingo), hotels (being demerged), paperboards and agribusiness. Consistent high-dividend payer since decades.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical
holdings.description: India's largest pharma company. US specialty dermatology leadership with Ilumya and Cequa. Taro Pharmaceutical in Canada and US. Strong domestic branded generics and emerging markets presence in 100+ countries.

holdings.name: Wipro
holdings.description: Azim Premji's IT company under transformation. Strategic acquisitions: Capco (BFSI consulting), Rizing (SAP), Edgile (cybersecurity). Consumer care (Santoor, Wipro Baby) remains a profitable and growing side business.

index.name: Nifty IT Index
index.description: Tracks Indian IT sector companies on NSE. India's IT sector generates $250B+ in annual exports employing 5.4M professionals. Index influenced by INR/USD exchange rates, US tech spending cycles, BFSI demand, and digital transformation adoption. Generative AI is providing a once-in-a-decade demand tailwind for Indian IT services.

holdings.name: TCS
holdings.description: India's largest IT company by revenue and market cap. Serves 1,200+ clients across 150 locations in 50+ countries. Market leader in BFSI, retail and manufacturing verticals. Generative AI deals ramping rapidly — over 100 AI-driven large deal wins announced in FY25.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Global IT pioneer with Cobalt cloud platform. Strong in consulting-led large deals — $4B+ TCV quarterly. Carbon-neutral operations since 2020. AI-first strategy with Infosys Topaz platform for enterprise AI transformation.

holdings.name: HCL Technologies
holdings.description: Products-led IT company with HCL Software generating $650M+ annual recurring revenue. IBM software IP portfolio acquisition a game-changer. Strong ER&D services in auto, aerospace and semiconductors.

holdings.name: Wipro
holdings.description: Diversified IT services transforming with consulting-led strategy. Capco (BFSI), Rizing (SAP), Edgile (Cyber) acquisitions elevate margins. 230,000+ employees across 65 countries. Wipro Enterprise AI platform launched.

holdings.name: Tech Mahindra
holdings.description: Telecom-focused IT company under Project Reboot transformation. 5G software and infrastructure a key differentiator. TechM Business Boost AI platform for enterprise clients. Diversifying into BFSI, healthcare and manufacturing.

holdings.name: LTIMindtree
holdings.description: Merged entity of L&T Infotech and Mindtree creating a $4B+ revenue IT major. Strong in BFSI, hi-tech, media and manufacturing. Canvas AI platform driving digital transformation deals across Fortune 500 clients.

holdings.name: L&T Technology Services
holdings.description: Pure-play engineering R&D services company. Strong in automotive EV (35% revenue), industrial, telecom and medical devices verticals. Beneficiary of global ER&D spend rising to $2.4T by 2030.

holdings.name: Mphasis
holdings.description: Deep BFSI-focused IT company with DXC Technology lineage. AI-first with DeepInsights platform. Strong US mortgage and banking sector exposure. CEO Nitin Rakesh driving hyper-personalization and cloud-first mandate.

holdings.name: Persistent Systems
holdings.description: Fastest-growing large mid-cap IT company. Specializes in product engineering, Salesforce, and cloud-native development. Salesforce Partner of the Year multiple times. 25%+ revenue growth for 8+ consecutive quarters.

holdings.name: Coforge
holdings.description: Formerly NIIT Technologies. Transformed into a high-growth IT services company. Deep vertical expertise in travel, BFSI and insurance. Xceltrait and SLK acquisition expanding US market. Strong deal momentum at $1B+ TCV.

index.name: Domestic Gold Price
index.description: Gold ETFs invest in 99.5% pure physical gold held in secure vaults. Each unit is backed by approximately 1 gram of gold. Unlike physical gold, there are zero making charges, no storage risk and no purity concerns. NAV tracks domestic gold prices influenced by international spot price (USD), INR/USD exchange rate and import duties. India's first Gold ETF was launched in 2007 by Benchmark AMC.

holdings.name: Physical Gold (99.5%)
holdings.description: 24-karat gold of 99.5% purity stored in secure insured vaults with HSBC/SBI as custodians. Each ETF unit represents approximately 1 gram of gold. SEBI-mandated regular independent audits. Zero counterparty risk — fully backed by actual physical metal.

holdings.name: Cash & Liquid Instruments
holdings.description: Short-term T-bills and overnight instruments held for liquidity management and redemption purposes. Maintained at minimum levels to maximize gold price exposure for investors.

holdings.name: Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGB)
holdings.description: Government of India bonds backed by gold, issued by RBI with 2.5% annual interest and sovereign guarantee. Permitted as a substitute for physical gold up to 10% of the scheme. Matures at prevailing gold price.

index.name: Domestic Gold Price
index.description: India's largest and most liquid Gold ETF. GOLDBEES was launched as one of India's first Gold ETFs in 2007 by Benchmark AMC (now Nippon India). Each unit approximately equals 1 gram of physical gold stored in HSBC custody vaults. Extremely popular among retail investors for hassle-free gold exposure without physical gold risks.

holdings.name: Physical Gold (99.5%)
holdings.description: Physical gold bullion of 99.5% purity stored in HSBC custody vaults in Mumbai. One of the most audited and transparent gold holdings in India. Regular NSDL/CDSL audit trail ensures unit holders confidence in the backing.

holdings.name: Cash & CBLO
holdings.description: Overnight money market instruments (Collateralized Borrowing & Lending Obligations and T-bills) to meet redemption obligations. Kept minimal to maximize gold exposure at all times.

index.name: Domestic Gold Price
index.description: HDFC Gold ETF offers investors exposure to gold price without holding physical gold. Each unit is backed by high-purity gold stored with Deutsche Bank custodian. A convenient and cost-efficient way to include gold in a diversified investment portfolio, recommended as 5-10% of total portfolio allocation.

holdings.name: Physical Gold (99.5%)
holdings.description: Physical gold of 99.5% purity held with Deutsche Bank Mumbai custody. Fully insured. SEBI audit compliance ensures unit holder assets are safe and segregated from AMC assets at all times.

holdings.name: Cash & Money Market
holdings.description: Money market instruments including T-bills and CPs held for day-to-day operational liquidity and redemption management.

index.name: Nifty Pharma Index
index.description: Tracks Indian pharmaceutical sector — world's 3rd largest by volume, 14th by value. India is the 'pharmacy of the world', supplying 20% of global generic medicines and 60% of global vaccine supply. Key drivers: US FDA approvals, branded domestic generics growth, biosimilars pipeline, complex specialty generics and CDMO services. India's cost advantage (60-70% cheaper than US/Europe) and scientific talent provide structural advantage.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical
holdings.description: India's largest pharma company by market cap (₹3.5 lakh crore). US specialty dermatology leader — Ilumya (psoriasis) and Cequa (dry eyes) are blockbusters. Taro Pharmaceutical in Canada/US. Strong domestic branded generics in 100+ countries.

holdings.name: Dr. Reddy's Labs
holdings.description: Pioneer of India's generics-to-global strategy. $3B+ revenue company. Complex generics and biosimilars (gRevlimid launch). Exceptional API manufacturing with backward integration. Founded by Dr. K. Anji Reddy in 1984 with ₹25 lakh.

holdings.name: Cipla
holdings.description: Making life-saving drugs affordable since 1935. Cipla's HIV drug pricing advocacy saved millions of lives in Africa in the 2000s. Leader in respiratory inhalers (Seroflo, Foracort), anti-infectives and consumer wellness via Cipla Health (Nasivion, Cipladine).

holdings.name: Divis Laboratories
holdings.description: India's top API and nutraceuticals contract manufacturer. Supplies to 9 of world's top 10 global pharma companies. Pristine USFDA compliance record across all plants. Fully backward integrated, debt-free balance sheet and 30%+ EBITDA margins.

holdings.name: Aurobindo Pharma
holdings.description: Major US generics player with 800+ ANDA filings, largest injectables portfolio among Indian pharma. API-to-formulations integration provides ~30% cost advantage. Strong oncology and penicillin segment. Aurovida Formulations for biosimilars.

holdings.name: Lupin Ltd
holdings.description: Specialty generics company. US complex generics include gSpiriva (respiratory) and gFostair. India branded generics in cardiology, CNS, diabetes. Growing biosimilars pipeline — etanercept, adalimumab under development for US and EU.

holdings.name: Abbott India
holdings.description: Indian subsidiary of Abbott Laboratories USA. Premium branded prescription medicines. Thyronorm is India's largest-selling thyroid brand. Cremaffin, Duphaston, Digene are household medicines. Consistent compounder at 20%+ ROCE.

holdings.name: Torrent Pharmaceuticals
holdings.description: Branded generics powerhouse. India dominance in cardiovascular (Shelcal, Nikoran) and CNS (Nexito). Unichem India acquisition doubled domestic scale. Germany and Brazil branded operations contributing stable international revenue.

holdings.name: Zydus Lifesciences
holdings.description: Ahmedabad-based pharma innovator. Launched ZyCoV-D — world's first DNA-based COVID vaccine. Strong US generics and complex generics (transdermal patches). Lipaglyn — world's first glitazar for diabetes approved in India.

holdings.name: Ipca Laboratories
holdings.description: Specialty API and formulations company. Largest producer of anti-malarial APIs globally. Strong branded generics in cardiovascular and musculoskeletal. Europe business growing rapidly post-compliance improvements.

index.name: Nifty India Defence Index
index.description: Tracks companies engaged in Indian defence manufacturing and services. India's defence budget stands at ₹6.21 lakh crore in FY25. Indigenization mandate requires 75% of defence procurement from domestic industry under IDDM policy. India targets $5B in defence exports by 2025. Massive multi-year order backlogs provide unprecedented revenue visibility for both PSU and private defence players.

holdings.name: HAL (Hindustan Aeronautics)
holdings.description: India's only aerospace manufacturing PSU. Makes Tejas LCA Mk1A fighter jets (83 aircraft ordered), ALH Dhruv helicopters, Dornier aircraft and servicing of MiG-21, Sukhoi-30 fleets. Order backlog of ₹90,000+ Cr from IAF, Army and Navy with 7+ year visibility.

holdings.name: BEL (Bharat Electronics)
holdings.description: Defence electronics PSU making Akash missile radar systems, submarine sonar, BMS (Battlefield Management System), electronic warfare and communication equipment. ₹65,000+ Cr order backlog. Exports to 20+ countries.

holdings.name: L&T
holdings.description: L&T's defence division excels in naval systems, armoured vehicles, K9 Vajra self-propelled howitzers, and NGARM anti-radiation missiles with DRDO. Building India's first private-sector submarine under Project-75 India Scorpene variant.

holdings.name: Bharat Forge
holdings.description: World's 2nd largest forging company now a serious defence player. Makes ATAGS 155mm/52-calibre artillery guns, armoured vehicle hulls, munitions and aerospace structural components. 20% revenue from defence with aggressive growth plans.

holdings.name: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders
holdings.description: Mumbai-based premier naval shipbuilder. Building Project-75 Scorpene submarines (6 delivered, more contracted), Project-15B destroyers and stealth frigates. ₹50,000+ Cr orderbook with 5+ year revenue visibility. Monopoly in submarine building.

holdings.name: Garden Reach Shipbuilders
holdings.description: Kolkata-based naval shipbuilder. Builds frigates (Project-17A), survey vessels, landing craft and fast patrol vessels. First Indian shipyard to export warships — delivered vessels to Mauritius (CGS Barracuda) and Bangladesh Navy.

holdings.name: BEML Ltd
holdings.description: Makes metro rail cars (Mumbai, Delhi, Bengaluru, Pune), mining equipment (dumpers, draglines) and high-mobility vehicles for Indian Army. Exporting metro coaches to Singapore MRT. Also makes helicopter ground support equipment.

holdings.name: Paras Defence
holdings.description: Niche defence-tech company. Makes electro-optic systems for missiles and helicopters, star trackers for ISRO/DRDO satellites, image intensifier tubes for night-vision goggles. India's only manufacturer of several critical optics components.

holdings.name: Data Patterns
holdings.description: Chennai-based defence and aerospace electronics company. Makes radar signal processing systems, avionics, missile seekers and electronic warfare modules. Works on DRDO programs including Tejas, Pinaka and Astra missile systems.

holdings.name: Solar Industries India
holdings.description: India's largest manufacturer of industrial explosives and defence ammunition. Making 155mm artillery shells, hand grenades, rockets and warheads. Sole supplier to Indian Army for several warhead types. Massive export orders from the US and Europe post-Ukraine conflict.

index.name: Nifty Next 50
index.description: Tracks companies ranked 51-100 by free-float market cap on NSE — often called the 'feeder index' because many constituents graduate to the Nifty 50. Combines large-cap stability with higher growth potential. Historically outperformed the Nifty 50 over long periods. Sector composition is more diversified than Nifty 50 with higher weight to consumption, real estate and healthcare.

holdings.name: Adani Enterprises
holdings.description: Flagship incubation platform of Adani Group. Operates 7 Indian airports (Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Lucknow, Jaipur, Guwahati, Thiruvananthapuram, Mangaluru), national highway roads, data centers and solar panel manufacturing in India.

holdings.name: Zomato
holdings.description: India's largest food delivery platform with 250M+ users and 200,000+ restaurant partners. Blinkit quick-commerce acquisition adding grocery delivery. Turned profitable in FY24. Hyperpure (B2B food supply) and District app expanding ecosystem.

holdings.name: DLF Ltd
holdings.description: India's largest listed real estate developer. DLF Cyber City in Gurugram houses hundreds of Fortune 500 MNC offices. Premium residential (The Camellias sold at ₹100 Cr/unit), commercial and retail properties. DCCDL REIT generates stable annuity income.

holdings.name: Pidilite Industries
holdings.description: Creator of Fevicol — India's most recognized brand worth $1B+. Dr. Fixit waterproofing, Fevikwik instant adhesive, M-Seal epoxy, Araldite. Dominant in adhesives with 350,000+ dealer network. Expanding into construction chemicals globally.

holdings.name: Godrej Consumer Products
holdings.description: Good Knight (India's #1 mosquito repellent), Cinthol soap, HIT spray. Africa acquisition (Godrej Africa) adds household insecticide revenue. Indonesia business (Megasari) growing fast. Target: 2x market growth rates every year.

holdings.name: Havells India
holdings.description: Premium electrical and consumer durables brand. Havells switchgear, cables and fans are No.1 or No.2 in respective categories. Lloyd ACs and washing machines targeting Voltas and LG. 15,000+ dealer network across India.

holdings.name: Vedanta Ltd
holdings.description: Natural resources major. Hindustan Zinc (67% owned) — world's largest integrated zinc-lead-silver producer. Aluminium, iron ore, oil & gas and copper operations. Anil Agarwal's Vedanta Group. High dividend payouts to parent.

holdings.name: Siemens India
holdings.description: Indian subsidiary of Siemens AG Germany. Factory automation (PLCs, drives), smart infrastructure (buildings, grids), rail electrification and digitalization solutions. Direct beneficiary of India's ₹11 lakh crore capex supercycle.

holdings.name: Torrent Pharmaceuticals
holdings.description: Branded generics powerhouse. Cardiovascular (Shelcal, Nikoran), CNS (Nexito, Libotryp) and diabetes franchise. Unichem India acquisition completed. Germany and Brazil branded operations contribute 40% of international revenue.

holdings.name: Interglobe Aviation
holdings.description: IndiGo Airlines — India's largest airline by capacity with 60%+ domestic market share. 300+ aircraft, 100+ destinations. Ultra-low-cost model and highest on-time performance in India. Fleet expansion with 970 Airbus A320 family aircraft on order.

holdings.name: Trent Ltd
holdings.description: Tata Group's retail powerhouse. Westside department stores (fashion, home). Zudio — India's fastest-growing value fashion chain with 500+ stores at ₹200-800 price points. Star Bazaar hypermarkets. Inditex JV for Zara India.

holdings.name: Bajaj Holdings
holdings.description: Core holding company of the Bajaj Group. Holds significant stakes in Bajaj Auto, Bajaj Finserv, and Bajaj Finance. Sum-of-parts valuation significantly below intrinsic value creating a structural discount play on the Bajaj conglomerate.

index.name: Domestic Silver Price
index.description: Silver ETFs invest in 99.9% pure physical silver stored in LBMA-certified secure vaults. Each unit backed by physical silver bullion. Silver offers dual demand — industrial (electronics, EV batteries, solar panels) and investment. Silver's use in solar cells makes it a green energy play. India is world's largest silver importer. More volatile than gold but with a lower entry price point.

holdings.name: Physical Silver (99.9%)
holdings.description: High-purity silver bullion of 99.9% fineness stored in LBMA-certified insured vaults. SEBI-mandated regular independent audits ensure unit-holder protection. Growing industrial demand from solar PV (120mg silver per panel) and EV batteries is a structural tailwind.

holdings.name: Cash & Equivalents
holdings.description: Overnight instruments and T-bills maintained for liquidity and daily redemption management. Kept minimal to maximize silver price exposure for investors at all times.

index.name: CRISIL Liquid Fund Index
index.description: India's first and largest liquid ETF — launched in 2003. Provides instant exchange-traded liquidity with money market returns. Invests in T-bills, commercial papers and certificates of deposit with maturity up to 91 days. Popular with traders and arbitrageurs for parking idle cash overnight. Pays daily dividend (reinvested as units), effectively functioning as a savings account that trades on NSE.

holdings.name: Treasury Bills (91-day)
holdings.description: Short-term Government of India securities with zero credit risk. 91-day T-bills auctioned by RBI weekly. Sovereign backing provides absolute safety. Yields track RBI repo rate with minimal duration risk.

holdings.name: Commercial Paper — AAA
holdings.description: Top-rated (A1+) unsecured promissory notes issued by blue-chip corporates like Reliance, TCS, HDFC and PSU entities. Short tenure of 15-90 days. Higher yield than T-bills (~25-50 bps) with minimal credit risk.

holdings.name: Certificate of Deposit
holdings.description: Negotiable money market instruments issued by scheduled commercial banks (HDFC, ICICI, SBI, Kotak). 7-91 day tenure. RBI-regulated and highly liquid. Credit risk very low given systemic importance of issuing banks.

holdings.name: Cash & Tri-party Repo
holdings.description: Overnight tri-party repos on government securities and CBLO (Collateralized Borrowing & Lending Obligations) for same-day settlement, daily operational liquidity and buffer for unexpected large redemptions.

index.name: Nifty CPSE Index
index.description: Launched in 2014 as part of Government of India's Disinvestment program — the first ETF used for government stake sales. Tracks Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs) — government-owned companies. The government has raised over ₹40,000 Cr through CPSE ETF tranches. Low expense ratio of 0.01% makes it the cheapest equity ETF in India. Typically offered at a discount during NFO tranches.

holdings.name: ONGC
holdings.description: Oil and Natural Gas Corporation — India's largest oil producer. Produces 70% of India's domestic crude oil and 62% of natural gas. Government holds 58% stake. Consistent dividend payer with high dividend yield. Owns HPCL (downstream) and ONGC Videsh for overseas exploration.

holdings.name: Coal India
holdings.description: World's largest coal mining company. Produces 700+ million tonnes annually — monopoly in Indian coal sector. Government holds 63% stake. Dividend yield of 6-8%. Essential for India's thermal power generation and energy security.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: India's largest power generation utility with 73 GW operational capacity. Government holds 51% stake. Transitioning to renewables: 60 GW renewable target by 2032. Regulated returns, consistent dividends and government backing provide stability.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corp
holdings.description: India's monopoly inter-state power transmission utility. Government holds 51%. Owns 70% of India's inter-state transmission network (175,000+ circuit km). Regulated 15.5% post-tax returns. Building green energy corridors.

holdings.name: Bharat Electronics
holdings.description: Defence electronics PSU making radar, sonar, communication and electronic warfare systems. Government holds 51.14%. ₹65,000+ Cr order backlog with 5+ year visibility. Growing export business to SE Asia and Middle East.

holdings.name: GAIL India
holdings.description: India's largest natural gas marketing and transmission company. 12,000+ km cross-country pipeline network. Government holds 51%. Beneficiary of India's city gas distribution (CGD) expansion and natural gas economy push.

holdings.name: Indian Oil Corporation
holdings.description: India's largest oil company by revenue. 11 refineries (80 MMTPA capacity), 33,000+ fuel stations, Indane LPG and SERVO lubricants brands. Government holds 52%. India's highest revenue company and Fortune 500 entrant.

holdings.name: BHEL
holdings.description: Bharat Heavy Electricals — India's largest power equipment manufacturer. Makes turbines, generators, transformers and boilers. Government holds 63%. Reviving order book from thermal power and defence electronics diversification.

holdings.name: Container Corp of India
holdings.description: India's largest container rail logistics company operating 89 rail terminals across India. Government holds 54% via Ministry of Railways. Beneficiary of dedicated freight corridors (DFC) improving container rail efficiency.

index.name: Nifty FMCG Index
index.description: Tracks Fast Moving Consumer Goods companies — a defensive sector with strong pricing power. Benefits from rising disposable incomes, aspirational rural consumption growth and favorable demographics (900M below-40 consumers). Includes personal care, home care, packaged foods, beverages, tobacco and ayurvedic products. Sector has delivered 13% CAGR over 20 years.

holdings.name: Hindustan Unilever
holdings.description: India's largest FMCG company with ₹61,000 Cr annual revenue. 50+ brands: Surf Excel, Rin, Dove, Lux, Lifebuoy, Knorr, Horlicks, Kissan. Present in 9 out of 10 Indian homes. Parent Unilever holds 61.9% stake.

holdings.name: ITC Ltd
holdings.description: Diversified conglomerate. Cigarettes (Gold Flake, Classic, Wills) contribute 60% profit. FMCG brands: Aashirvaad (atta, spices), Sunfeast (biscuits), Yippee (noodles), Bingo (snacks). Hotels being demerged. Dividend aristocrat.

holdings.name: Nestle India
holdings.description: Indian arm of Swiss giant Nestle AG. Maggi noodles (58% market share), KitKat, Nescafe, Munch, Milkmaid dominate their segments. Premium pricing power and fortress balance sheet. 120+ year Indian presence (est. 1912).

holdings.name: Britannia Industries
holdings.description: India's biscuit market leader with 34% share. Good Day, Tiger, Marie Gold, NutriChoice, Bourbon are household favorites. 5M+ outlet distribution. Wadia Group-controlled. Expanding into dairy (Pure Magic cheese), breads (Modern) and international.

holdings.name: Dabur India
holdings.description: India's largest ayurvedic FMCG company. Dabur Chyawanprash (66% market share), Real juice (50% share), Vatika hair oil, Hajmola digestive. 8M+ outlets distribution. Strong rural push. International business in 100+ countries contributes 28% revenue.

holdings.name: Godrej Consumer Products
holdings.description: Good Knight mosquito repellent (India's #1), Cinthol soap, Godrej No.1, HIT insect killer. Africa (Megasari) and Indonesia operations contribute 30% revenue. 2x market growth rate target from premiumisation and distribution deepening.

holdings.name: Tata Consumer Products
holdings.description: Tata Tea (India's #1 branded tea), Tata Salt (India's #1 salt), Tata Sampann pulses and spices. Tetley internationally (world's #2 tea brand). Starbucks JV (350+ cafes). NourishCo (Himalayan, Tata Gluco Plus beverages).

holdings.name: Marico Ltd
holdings.description: Parachute coconut oil commands 59% market share. Saffola edible oils (heart-health platform). Set Wet, Livon hair care. Bangladesh and Vietnam international operations contribute 22% revenue. Consistent 18%+ ROCE compounder.

holdings.name: Colgate-Palmolive India
holdings.description: India's largest oral care company with 50%+ toothpaste market share. Colgate Strong Teeth, Colgate Sensitive, Palmolive soaps. Parent Colgate-Palmolive US holds 51%. High ROCE of 60%+. Consistently among India's most trusted brands.

index.name: Nifty Auto Index
index.description: Tracks automobile and auto component companies. Cyclical sector benefiting from rising incomes, premiumisation, vehicle electrification, and export growth. India is the world's 3rd largest auto market and #1 in 2-wheelers. Includes passenger vehicles, 2-wheelers, commercial vehicles, tractors and auto ancillaries. EV transition is accelerating across all segments.

holdings.name: Maruti Suzuki
holdings.description: India's largest car maker with 41%+ domestic market share. JV with Suzuki Japan since 1982. Dominates entry and mid-segments: Alto, Swift, Baleno, Dzire, Ertiga, Grand Vitara. Transitioning to strong hybrid (Invicto, Fronx) and electric (eVitara) platforms.

holdings.name: Mahindra & Mahindra
holdings.description: SUV market leader with blockbusters: XUV 3XO, XUV700, Scorpio-N, Thar. Farmtrac tractor dominance (40%+ domestic share). Electric: BE 6e and XEV 9e for premium EVs. Last-mile electric delivery (Treo, Zor) growing fast.

holdings.name: Tata Motors
holdings.description: Owns Jaguar Land Rover (contributes 70% revenue, 80% profit). Domestic PV revival: Nexon, Punch (top-10 bestsellers), Nexon EV (India's best-selling EV). Tata Ace and Prima CV franchise. Turnaround under N Chandrasekaran.

holdings.name: Bajaj Auto
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest 2W maker. Pulsar, Dominar, CT, Platina brands. 40% export contribution — dominates Africa and Latin America. CNG bike Bajaj Freedom 125 — world's first CNG motorcycle. Electric: Chetak scooter relaunched successfully.

holdings.name: Eicher Motors
holdings.description: Royal Enfield — India's premium motorcycle brand with 90% share in 250cc+ premium segment. Himalayan 450 and Hunter 350 driving new buyer acquisition. VE Commercial Vehicles JV with Volvo Group for trucks, buses. Expanding in Southeast Asia.

holdings.name: TVS Motor Company
holdings.description: India's 3rd largest 2W maker. Apache RTR series and Jupiter scooter are best-sellers. TVS iQube electric scooter gaining share. Acquired Norton Motorcycles UK for premium brand. Strong exports to 60+ countries.

holdings.name: Bosch India
holdings.description: Indian arm of Robert Bosch GmbH Germany. Leader in fuel injection systems (80%+ share), anti-lock braking (ABS), powertrain electrification and automotive electronics. Beneficiary of BS-VI upgrade, hybrid and EV transitions.

holdings.name: Motherson Wiring
holdings.description: Global auto components major. Wiring harnesses (India's largest), exterior mirrors, bumpers, plastic interior modules. Supplies to Mercedes-Benz, BMW, VW, Toyota globally. India's largest auto ancillary exporter at $19B+ revenue.

holdings.name: Hero MotoCorp
holdings.description: World's largest 2-wheeler manufacturer by volume. Splendor (world's best-selling bike), HF Deluxe, Passion dominate entry segment. Xtreme 160R premium series growing. Vida electric scooter for EV segment. Split from Honda in 2011.

holdings.name: Bharat Forge
holdings.description: World's 2nd largest forging company. Makes crankshafts, front axle beams, connecting rods for PV, CV and 2W makers globally. Customer includes Ford, Daimler, Volvo. Pivoting to defence (ATAGS artillery guns) and aerospace.

name: Nippon India ETF Nifty PSU Bank

index.name: Nifty PSU Bank Index
index.description: Tracks 12 public sector banks in India — institutions owned by the Government of India. Post-2019 NPA cleanup (after massive recapitalization of ₹3.5 lakh crore by GoI) dramatically improved asset quality. ROE of PSU banks has recovered from sub-5% to 12-15%. Beneficiaries of India's strong credit growth cycle, priority sector mandates and government-sponsored schemes like PM Mudra, PM Jan Dhan and PM SVANidhi.

holdings.name: State Bank of India
holdings.description: India's largest bank — 23% of total banking deposits. ₹55 lakh crore in total assets. 22,500+ branches and 65,000+ ATMs. YONO super-app has 40M+ users processing millions of transactions daily. Dominant in MSME, agri, home and education loans.

holdings.name: Punjab National Bank
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest PSU bank post-merger with Oriental Bank of Commerce and United Bank of India (2020). Founded in Lahore in 1894. Post-NPA cleanup ROE recovering. Strong Punjab, UP and Bihar presence. 10,000+ branches.

holdings.name: Bank of Baroda
holdings.description: India's most international bank with operations in 100+ countries. Merged with Vijaya Bank and Dena Bank in 2019. Strong corporate, agri and overseas banking. bob World digital app. High dividend yield with improving ROE.

holdings.name: Canara Bank
holdings.description: Largest PSU bank in South India, headquartered in Bengaluru. Post-Syndicate Bank merger in 2020 increased scale significantly. 10,000+ branches. Strong MSME and agriculture lending. Improving NPA ratios and ROA trajectory.

holdings.name: Union Bank of India
holdings.description: Post triple-merger entity absorbing Andhra Bank and Corporation Bank (2020). Mumbai-based with 9,000+ branches. Focus on retail, agriculture, MSME and government scheme lending. Improving profitability with GNPA below 5%.

holdings.name: Bank of India
holdings.description: One of India's oldest PSU banks founded in 1906 in Mumbai. Strong Maharashtra and Gujarat presence. 5,100+ branches domestically with international presence in 20+ countries. Improving credit cost trajectory driving profitability recovery.

holdings.name: Indian Bank
holdings.description: Chennai-headquartered PSU bank post-merger with Allahabad Bank (2020). Strong South India base with 6,000+ branches. Focus on agriculture, MSME and retail lending. INDOASIS digital banking platform driving NRI remittances.

holdings.name: UCO Bank
holdings.description: Kolkata-headquartered public sector bank. Strong eastern India presence particularly West Bengal and Bihar. Participates in all GoI schemes. RuPay card promotion, PM Jan Dhan accounts. Steady improvement in asset quality post recapitalisation.

holdings.name: Bank of Maharashtra
holdings.description: Pune-headquartered PSU bank. Best performer among medium PSU banks in recent years. One of the first to report GNPA below 3%. Strong Maharashtra presence. Consistent retail deposit growth from tier-2 and tier-3 cities.

index.name: Nifty Midcap 150
index.description: Represents 150 midcap companies ranked 101-250 by free-float market cap. Higher growth potential than large caps with reasonable liquidity. Historically outperformed Nifty 50 by 3-4% annually over long periods. Suitable for investors with 5-7 year investment horizon and moderate-to-high risk appetite. Sector composition is more diversified and captures India's domestic consumption and manufacturing boom.

holdings.name: Trent Ltd
holdings.description: Tata Group's retail powerhouse. Zudio — India's fastest-growing value fashion chain (500+ stores). Westside department stores for premium fashion. Star Bazaar hypermarkets. Inditex JV for Zara in India. 35%+ revenue CAGR making it a structural growth story.

holdings.name: Dixon Technologies
holdings.description: India's largest EMS (Electronics Manufacturing Services) company. Makes LED TVs (for Samsung, Panasonic), mobile phones (for Motorola, Nokia), washing machines, LED lighting. Pure-play on India's PLI scheme and Make in India electronics manufacturing.

holdings.name: Prestige Estates
holdings.description: Bangalore-based premium real estate developer. Builds residential (prestige city), commercial offices, retail malls and hospitality properties. Strong brand in South India's property market. NCR expansion adding new growth leg.

holdings.name: Kaynes Technology
holdings.description: Electronic Manufacturing Services company focusing on IoT, industrial electronics and automotive embedded electronics. One of India's fastest-growing EMS players benefiting from domestic electronics production growth and import substitution.

holdings.name: Jindal Steel & Power
holdings.description: Integrated steel producer (9 MMTPA capacity) with captive iron ore mines in Odisha and coal mines. Operates India's largest single-location blast furnace at Angul, Odisha. Beneficiary of infrastructure capex and PLI for specialty steel.

holdings.name: Voltas Ltd
holdings.description: Tata Group's cooling products and engineering company. India's #1 AC brand by market share (22%). Also into engineering, electro-mechanical projects (MEP in buildings) and refrigeration. Voltas Beko JV for washing machines and refrigerators.

holdings.name: Tube Investments of India
holdings.description: Murugappa Group flagship. Metal formed products (tubes, cylinders for auto). TI Cycles (BSA, Hercules, Montra brands). Acquired CG Power (power equipment). New-age mobility investments through Cellestial E-Mobility and Vidhyut-Tech EV drivetrains.

holdings.name: Indian Hotels Company
holdings.description: Tata Group's hospitality arm. Taj Hotels — India's most iconic luxury hotel brand. Vivanta (upscale), SeleQtions, and Ginger (budget). 250+ hotels in 100+ locations globally. Strong RevPAR recovery post-COVID and robust MICE segment.

holdings.name: Max Financial Services
holdings.description: Parent company of Max Life Insurance — India's 4th largest private life insurer. Strong bancassurance channel via Axis Bank JV. Term and ULIP products growing. Embedded value (EV) growth of 20%+ driving shareholder value creation.

holdings.name: Coromandel International
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest phosphatic fertiliser company. Makes DAP, NPK fertilisers, specialty nutrients, crop protection chemicals and organic nutrients. Strong South India distribution. Murugappa Group company with consistent 20%+ ROE.

holdings.name: Bharat Forge
holdings.description: World's 2nd largest forging company. Makes crankshafts, front axle beams and connecting rods for global auto OEMs. Pivoting to high-margin defence (ATAGS artillery guns) and aerospace components. 40% revenue from exports.

holdings.name: Page Industries
holdings.description: Exclusive licensee of Jockey brand in India, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh and UAE since 1994. India's premium innerwear market leader. Also holds Speedo license for India. Asset-light franchise model with 80%+ ROCE and premium brand positioning.

index.name: Nifty Energy Index
index.description: Covers oil & gas exploration, refining, marketing and power generation companies. India's energy demand is growing the fastest globally — India will become world's 3rd largest energy consumer by 2030. Index covers the full energy value chain: upstream (exploration), midstream (refining, transmission) and downstream (marketing). Energy transition themes: solar, hydrogen and clean fuels are transforming traditional energy companies.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: World's largest refining complex at Jamnagar (1.4 million barrels/day). O2C (Oil to Chemicals) business generates ₹6 lakh crore+ revenue. Investing ₹75,000 Cr in new energy (40 GW solar, green hydrogen at Dhirubhai Ambani Green Energy Giga Complex).

holdings.name: ONGC
holdings.description: India's largest oil and gas exploration company producing 70% of India's crude oil and 62% of natural gas. Government holds 58%. Owns 54% of HPCL. ONGC Videsh has overseas assets in 15+ countries. Dividend yield of 5-6%.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: India's largest power generator — 73 GW operational, 30 GW under construction. Targets 60 GW renewable by 2032 (solar parks, wind, hydro). Government holds 51%. High dividend yield, regulated returns, AAA-rated.

holdings.name: Coal India
holdings.description: World's largest coal mining company. Produces 700+ MT annually — monopoly in Indian coal sector with 7 subsidiaries. Government holds 63%. Dividend yield of 6-8%. Essential for India's 200 GW thermal power generation base.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corp
holdings.description: India's monopoly inter-state power transmission utility — 70% of India's grid. 175,000+ circuit km of HV lines, 264 substations. Government holds 51%. Regulated 15.5% post-tax returns. Building Green Energy Corridors for 50 GW solar.

holdings.name: Indian Oil Corporation
holdings.description: India's largest oil company by revenue — Fortune 500 ranked company. 11 refineries (82 MMTPA combined capacity), 33,000+ IndianOil fuel stations, Indane LPG (10 crore+ households) and SERVO lubricants. Expanding into petrochemicals and green hydrogen.

holdings.name: BPCL
holdings.description: Bharat Petroleum — integrated oil company. 3 refineries (38 MMTPA), 19,000+ petrol pumps, 6,200 LPG distributors. Government holds 52.98%. Mega Bina refinery expansion underway. Strong petrochemicals growth roadmap.

holdings.name: GAIL India
holdings.description: India's largest natural gas marketing and transmission company. 12,000+ km cross-country pipeline network. City gas distribution via MGL, IGL and others. Beneficiary of India's gas economy push (current 6% of energy mix targeting 15% by 2030).

holdings.name: Adani Green Energy
holdings.description: India's largest renewable energy company with 10+ GW operational solar and wind capacity. Targeting 50 GW by 2030 as part of Adani Green's ambitious clean energy expansion. Secured India's largest single solar power order from SECI.

holdings.name: Torrent Power
holdings.description: Gujarat-based integrated power utility. Generation (4 GW+ gas, solar), transmission and distribution in Ahmedabad, Surat, Gandhinagar and Agra. One of India's most profitable private power distribution licensees. Strong renewable capacity addition track.

index.name: Nifty Infrastructure Index
index.description: Tracks infrastructure companies across power, telecom, roads, airports, ports, construction and building materials. Beneficiary of India's ₹111 lakh crore National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) and the government's capital expenditure of ₹11 lakh crore in FY26 Union Budget — India's largest-ever capex outlay. Infrastructure spending is the key driver of India's 7%+ GDP growth target.

holdings.name: Larsen & Toubro
holdings.description: India's largest EPC conglomerate. ₹4 lakh crore order book covering metros, highways, airports, nuclear power plants, warships and data centers. Exports defence equipment (K9 Vajra). LTIMindtree and L&T Technology Services add high-margin IT services.

holdings.name: Adani Ports & SEZ
holdings.description: India's largest private port operator. 13 ports handling 25%+ of India's cargo. Mundra (Gujarat) is India's largest container port. Logistics park network expanding via APSEZ. Benefiting from India's EXIM trade boom targeting $2T exports by 2030.

holdings.name: Bharti Airtel
holdings.description: India's largest telecom infrastructure provider. 380M+ subscribers. 5G rolled out in 5,000+ cities. Airtel Business: enterprise connectivity and cloud for 3,000+ corporates. Africa operations (Airtel Africa) across 14 countries add diversification.

holdings.name: UltraTech Cement
holdings.description: India's largest and world's 3rd largest cement company with 130 MT capacity. Aditya Birla Group flagship. 23+ integrated plants pan-India. Acquiring Kesoram and Heidelberg India to consolidate leadership. Pricing power in key Western and Southern markets.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: India's largest power generator (73 GW operational). Building India's largest single solar project (7.5 GW at Rajasthan). Targeting 130 GW total capacity by 2032 with 50% from renewables. NTPC Green Energy subsidiary for renewable assets.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corporation
holdings.description: Backbone of India's electricity grid. 70% of inter-state transmission. Building ultra-high-voltage DC corridors for renewable energy integration from Rajasthan and Gujarat solar/wind zones to demand centers. Regulated monopoly.

holdings.name: Adani Enterprises
holdings.description: Adani Group's incubation engine. 7 Indian airports (60M+ passengers/year), national highway roads, copper manufacturing (Mundra), data centers and solar panel factory (world's largest integrated solar module plant).

holdings.name: Ambuja Cements
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest cement group (post Adani acquisition of Holcim India). 78 MT capacity. Strong Western and Northern market presence. Synergy benefits from limestone mines, railways and port infrastructure of Adani Group.

holdings.name: Hindustan Construction Company
holdings.description: Pioneer in tunnelling, bridges, hydroelectric dams and nuclear power civil construction. Tehri Dam, Mumbai Metro, Zojila Tunnel. Largest hydro-power EPC contractor in India. Debt restructured, order book recovering strongly.

holdings.name: IRB Infrastructure
holdings.description: India's largest private roads developer with 20,000+ km of highway portfolio under HAM and BOT models. NHAI's premium partner for NH development. IRB InvIT generates stable toll fee income. 10+ GW operational road assets.

index.name: Nifty Next 50
index.description: Tracks Nifty Next 50 index — companies ranked 51-100. Often called 'the feeder to Nifty 50'. Companies that graduate from Next 50 to Nifty 50 deliver re-rating gains. Strong 10-year SIP returns historically outperform large-cap active funds.

holdings.name: Adani Enterprises
holdings.description: Flagship incubation platform of Adani Group. Operates airports (Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Lucknow), roads, data centers, solar manufacturing, and coal trading businesses.

holdings.name: DLF Ltd
holdings.description: India's largest real estate developer. Premium residential and commercial properties in NCR. DLF Cyber City REIT generates steady rental income. Launch pipeline strong.

holdings.name: Pidilite Industries
holdings.description: Fevicol creator with unassailable brand moat. Construction chemicals, adhesives, art materials. 70% revenue from Fevicol-related products. Premium valuations for dominant market position.

holdings.name: Havells India
holdings.description: Premium electrical and consumer durables. Havells switchgear and cables, Lloyd ACs. Strong brand and distribution. Targeting ₹20,000 Cr revenue.

holdings.name: Godrej Consumer
holdings.description: Good Knight, Cinthol, HIT brands dominate insecticides and personal care. Strong emerging markets presence in Africa and Indonesia.

holdings.name: Vedanta Ltd
holdings.description: Natural resources conglomerate. Zinc (via Hindustan Zinc), aluminium, iron ore, copper, oil & gas. High dividend payout. Exposure to commodity supercycle.

holdings.name: Siemens India
holdings.description: Beneficiary of India's manufacturing and infrastructure capex. Factory automation, smart infrastructure, mobility solutions. High-margin MNC with strong order book.

holdings.name: Zomato
holdings.description: India's largest food delivery platform. 250M+ users, 200K+ restaurants. Blinkit acquisition adds quick-commerce. Path to profitability achieved in 2024.

holdings.name: Trent Ltd
holdings.description: Tata Group's retail arm operating Westside, Zudio, and Star Bazaar. Zudio's value fashion has been a phenomenal growth story, expanding rapidly across India's tier-2 and tier-3 cities.

holdings.name: IndiGo (InterGlobe)
holdings.description: India's dominant airline with ~60% domestic market share. Ultra-low-cost model. Massive fleet expansion with 500+ aircraft on order. Beneficiary of India's aviation boom.

holdings.name: Bajaj Holdings
holdings.description: Investment holding company of the Bajaj Group. Holds stakes in Bajaj Auto, Bajaj Finserv, and Bajaj Finance. Trades at a structural discount to NAV, offering long-term value.

holdings.name: Info Edge (Naukri)
holdings.description: India's leading internet classifieds platform. Naukri dominates job portals, 99acres in real estate, Jeevansathi in matrimony. Investor in Zomato and PolicyBazaar at early stages.

index.name: Nifty Metal Index
index.description: Tracks the Nifty Metal index representing India's metals and mining sector. Beneficiary of global commodity cycles, infrastructure buildout, and China demand recovery. Covers steel, aluminium, zinc, copper, and diversified miners.

holdings.name: Tata Steel
holdings.description: India's largest integrated steel company. Tata Steel Europe (Corus) acquisition expanded global footprint. JhariaCoal mines for captive use. Beneficiary of infrastructure boom and auto sector demand.

holdings.name: Hindalco Industries
holdings.description: World's largest aluminium rolling company via Novelis (USA/Canada). Domestic copper smelting via Birlacopper. Novelis contributes 60%+ revenues and is the key re-rating driver.

holdings.name: JSW Steel
holdings.description: India's largest steel producer by capacity (27 MTPA target). Coastal plants enable imported coal advantage. Strong automotive and real estate demand. JSW One digital procurement platform.

holdings.name: Vedanta Ltd
holdings.description: Multi-metal conglomerate: zinc (Hindustan Zinc), aluminium, iron ore, copper, oil & gas. High dividend policy. Zinc business is world-class asset.

holdings.name: Hindustan Zinc
holdings.description: World's second-largest zinc-lead miner and producer. Rajpura Dariba and Sindesar Khurd mines. Rampura Agucha is one of the world's largest open-pit zinc mines. 65% owned by Vedanta.

holdings.name: SAIL (Steel Authority)
holdings.description: India's largest PSU steel company. Integrated plants at Bhilai, Rourkela, Bokaro, Durgapur, Burnpur. Government infra orders provide stable demand. Target 50 MTPA capacity by 2030.

holdings.name: APL Apollo Tubes
holdings.description: India's largest structural steel tubes manufacturer. Branded products command premium pricing. Building materials and infrastructure construction applications. Asset-light distribution model.

holdings.name: National Aluminium
holdings.description: India's only integrated bauxite-alumina-aluminium PSU. Captive power plants and Damanjodi alumina refinery. Low-cost producer with long-term export contracts.

holdings.name: Ratnamani Metals
holdings.description: Premium stainless steel and carbon steel pipes for oil & gas, fertilizers, power. High-margin specialty products. Strong order book from refinery and petrochemical capex.

holdings.name: Hindustan Copper
holdings.description: India's only vertically integrated copper producer. Khetri and Malanjkhand mines. Benefits from global copper demand surge driven by electric vehicles and renewable energy transition.

index.name: Nifty Realty Index
index.description: Tracks the Nifty Realty index covering India's listed real estate developers. Beneficiary of the residential real estate upcycle, office space demand, and mall/retail expansion. India's housing shortage of 18.78 million units underpins long-term demand.

holdings.name: DLF Ltd
holdings.description: India's largest real estate developer. DLF Cyber City REIT in Gurugram generates steady commercial income. New luxury residential launches — The Camellias, Arbour — seeing record bookings. Net debt reduction ongoing.

holdings.name: Macrotech (Lodha)
holdings.description: Premium residential developer. Lodha Park, Lodha Altamount in Mumbai command ultra-luxury pricing. Digital City township targets IT/ITeS workforce housing. Strong FY25 pre-sales momentum.

holdings.name: Godrej Properties
holdings.description: Godrej Group's real estate arm. Asset-light JDA model reduces capital risk. Strong brand commands pricing power. Active in NCR, Pune, Bengaluru, and Mumbai MMR.

holdings.name: Prestige Estates
holdings.description: South India's largest developer, now pan-India. Prestige Falcon City, Prestige City projects drive volumes. Hotel and office assets provide annuity income. Bengaluru market dominance.

holdings.name: Oberoi Realty
holdings.description: Ultra-premium developer in Mumbai. Oberoi Garden City in Goregaon, Elysian at Mulund. Commercial (Commerz III) and hospitality (Westin) provide recurring income. High margins on luxury sales.

holdings.name: Phoenix Mills
holdings.description: India's largest mall developer and operator. Phoenix Palladium (Mumbai), Phoenix Marketcity portfolio. Mall consumption growth driven by premiumisation of retail. Strong asset monetisation.

holdings.name: Sobha Ltd
holdings.description: Integrated construction and development model. Strong in Bengaluru and Kerala markets. Backward integration into interior furnishings and bathware. Solid corporate governance.

holdings.name: Brigade Enterprises
holdings.description: Diversified real estate in South India. Brigade Tech Gardens is a major IT park. Brigade Hotel group provides hospitality revenues. Active in Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Chennai.

holdings.name: Sunteck Realty
holdings.description: Mumbai-focused luxury and ultra-luxury developer. Sunteck City in BKC surrounds. Premium positioning targets HNI and NRI buyers. Low inventory overhang drives ASP growth.

holdings.name: Signature Global
holdings.description: Affordable housing specialist in NCR. PMAY beneficiary. Focus on Gurugram and Sohna Road micro-markets. Strong volumes driven by first-time homebuyer demand.

index.name: Nifty Healthcare Index
index.description: Tracks the Nifty Healthcare index covering pharma, hospitals, diagnostics, and healthcare services. India's healthcare sector is a structural multi-decade opportunity with chronic disease burden, hospital underpenetration, and pharma exports as tailwinds.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical
holdings.description: India's largest pharma company and top-5 globally in specialty generics. Taro Pharma (USA), Ilumya/Cequa specialty brands, Winlevi dermatology — building US specialty beyond generics. Strong R&D pipeline.

holdings.name: Dr Reddy's Laboratories
holdings.description: Hyderabad-based generic pharma giant. US generics, branded formulations in India and Russia, API manufacturing. Revlimid (lenalidomide) para-IV authorized generic provides multi-year earnings boost.

holdings.name: Cipla Ltd
holdings.description: Piramal Group's pharma division — India's third largest. Strong US inhalation business (Advair generics pipeline). Africa branded generics. Cipla brand dominates respiratory and HIV in India.

holdings.name: Divi's Laboratories
holdings.description: World's largest manufacturer of APIs for regulated markets. Key supplier to top 10 global pharma. Carotenoids business and nutraceuticals. Asset-light model with industry-leading EBITDA margins of 35%+.

holdings.name: Apollo Hospitals
holdings.description: India's largest private hospital network with 70+ hospitals. Apollo HealthCo digital platform and Apollo Pharmacy chain. Chronic disease management, oncology, and organ transplants as key specialities.

holdings.name: Max Healthcare
holdings.description: North India's premium hospital chain with 17 hospitals (3,400+ beds). Oncology Centre of Excellence. Max Smart Super Speciality Hospital, Saket is flagship. IPO proceeds funding brownfield expansion.

holdings.name: Fortis Healthcare
holdings.description: Multi-specialty hospital chain across India and overseas. IHH Healthcare (Malaysia) is majority owner providing capital. SRL Diagnostics integration strengthens pathology vertical.

holdings.name: Narayana Hrudalaya
holdings.description: Affordable cardiac care at scale. Rabindranath Tagore International Institute of Cardiac Sciences. Health City Cayman Islands offshore medical tourism. ₹5,000 open-heart surgery model.

holdings.name: Torrent Pharma
holdings.description: Gujarat-based pharma with strong branded domestic portfolio. Germany (Heumann Pharma) and US operations. Cardiovascular, CNS, and gastro therapies dominate India prescriptions.

holdings.name: Lupin Ltd
holdings.description: Complex generics specialist: inhalation (Spiriva generic), injectables, biosimilars (Etanercept). US and Japan market exposure. Domestic branded Lupin franchise in TB, cardiovascular, diabetology.

index.name: Nifty Private Bank Index
index.description: Tracks only private sector banks within the Nifty Banking universe. Excludes PSU banks (SBI, PNB, BOB). Private banks historically deliver higher ROE, better asset quality, and superior earnings growth due to technology investment and retail banking penetration.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: India's largest private bank post HDFC merger. ₹25 trillion balance sheet. Stable asset quality with GNPA ~1.3%. Largest home loan book via HDFC Ltd integration. Digital: 90M+ customers on mobile banking.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Second-largest private bank. Transformation under CEO Sandeep Bakhshi — NIMs expanded, opex controlled, ROE 17%+. Loan book well-diversified across retail, SME, and corporate. iMobile Pay has 9M+ users.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Premium private bank with one of the industry's best asset quality records. GNPA consistently below 2%. Uday Kotak's franchise known for conservative underwriting. Kotak 811 digital bank has 4M+ accounts.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: Third-largest private bank. Citibank India retail portfolio acquisition added 3M premium credit card customers. Ultra FD and premium banking products. ROE improving trajectory with credit cost normalisation.

holdings.name: IndusInd Bank
holdings.description: Wholesale and commercial banking with Bharat Financial Inclusion (microfinance) subsidiary. Vehicle financing market leader. Consumer finance growing rapidly. Mid-size bank with high NIM profile.

holdings.name: Federal Bank
holdings.description: Kerala-headquartered private bank with pan-India ambitions. NRI remittances from Gulf a key low-cost float source. Well-capitalized with strong retail and SME loan growth. Strong South India brand.

holdings.name: IDFC First Bank
holdings.description: Transformed from infrastructure NBFC to retail bank under V. Vaidyanathan. Zero-fee savings account and high-interest FD strategy. Retail loan growth, credit card and personal loan momentum strong.

holdings.name: Bandhan Bank
holdings.description: Microfinance-turned-bank serving rural and semi-urban India. West Bengal and Northeast concentration risk. Highest NIM in banking (~7%). Diversifying into housing finance and SME banking.

holdings.name: RBL Bank
holdings.description: Specialty banking in credit cards (co-branded with Bajaj Finance) and microfinance. Niche SME banking franchise. Turnaround story under new management post CEO transition in 2022.

holdings.name: Yes Bank
holdings.description: Reconstructed bank post 2020 RBI intervention. Now owned by SBI (26%) + consortium of banks. Clean balance sheet, improving operational metrics. Small weighting due to reduced free-float market cap.

name: Mirae Asset Nifty Financial Services ETF

index.name: Nifty Financial Services Index
index.description: Tracks the Nifty Financial Services index — the broadest financial sector index covering banks, NBFCs, insurance, housing finance, and wealth management. India's financial sector is a proxy for the entire economy's growth, driven by credit expansion, insurance penetration, and capital markets deepening.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Largest private bank. Post-HDFC merger balance sheet ₹25 trillion. Dominant retail, SME, and corporate banking franchise. Consistent 17-18% ROE over cycles.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Transformed retail-first strategy under CEO Bakhshi. NIMs above 4%, best-in-class credit cost. iGrow, merchant solutions, and open banking APIs driving new revenue streams.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Premium franchise with conservative lending. Kotak Securities, Kotak AMC, Kotak Insurance create a financial services conglomerate under one listed entity.

holdings.name: SBI Life Insurance
holdings.description: India's largest private life insurer by new business premium. SBI's branch network of 22,000+ provides unmatched distribution. ULIPs and term plans drive volumes.

holdings.name: HDFC Life Insurance
holdings.description: Premium private life insurer. Strong annuity, protection, and retirement product mix. PAR savings and non-PAR guaranteed products for risk-averse customers. Consistent VNB margin above 26%.

holdings.name: Bajaj Finance
holdings.description: India's most profitable NBFC. 90M+ customers. Consumer finance, SME lending, rural and commercial lending. Bajaj Pay and EMI Card ecosystem create customer stickiness.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: Post-Citi integration, axis has the largest credit card portfolio among private banks. Premium affluent segment banking growing rapidly.

holdings.name: ICICI Lombard
holdings.description: India's largest private general insurer. Motor, health, and commercial lines. Strong combined ratio management. Data-driven underwriting and claims. Acquired Bharti AXA General Insurance.

holdings.name: ICICI Prudential Life
holdings.description: Joint venture between ICICI Bank and Prudential plc. Strong ULIP franchise with equity savings and pension products. Protection business scale-up underway. APE growth driven by direct and banca channels.

holdings.name: Bajaj Finserv
holdings.description: Holding company for Bajaj Finance, Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance, and Bajaj Allianz General Insurance. Trading at structural conglomerate discount to sum-of-parts. Insurance businesses scaling rapidly.

index.name: Nifty MNC Index
index.description: Tracks multinationals listed on Indian exchanges where foreign promoter holds 50%+. MNCs bring superior corporate governance, technology transfer, global brand equity, and consistent dividend payouts. Historically lower volatility with steady compounding.

holdings.name: Nestle India
holdings.description: Indian arm of Swiss food giant. Maggi noodles (dominant 60% market share), KitKat, Munch, Milkmaid. Premiumisation via Starbucks RTD, Nesplus, and Milo. Consistent 25%+ EBITDA margins over decades.

holdings.name: Hindustan Unilever
holdings.description: Indian arm of Unilever. Surf Excel, Dove, Pond's, Lux, Kwality Wall's. 50+ brands across home care, personal care, food. GSK Consumer Healthcare merger added Horlicks, Boost.

holdings.name: Maruti Suzuki
holdings.description: Suzuki Motor Corp's Indian arm (58% stake). India's largest passenger vehicle maker with ~42% market share. Swift, Dzire, Baleno, Ertiga, Jimny, Grand Vitara. CNG vehicle leadership.

holdings.name: ABB India
holdings.description: Electrification, motion, process automation, and robotics. ABB Switzerland holds 75%. Key beneficiary of India's power T&D upgrade cycle, railways electrification, and green hydrogen projects.

holdings.name: Siemens India
holdings.description: Factory automation, smart infrastructure, rail systems, digital industries. Siemens AG holds 75%. India subsidiary wins large orders from NTPC, Railways, and L&T. High-margin engineering solutions.

holdings.name: Bosch India
holdings.description: Robert Bosch GmbH holds 70%. Automotive fuel systems, power tools, and home appliances. Transitioning from diesel fuel injection to EV components, ADAS systems, and hydrogen cell technology.

holdings.name: Honeywell Automation
holdings.description: Honeywell USA holds 75%. Process automation, building management, safety solutions. Chemical, refining, and pharma industries drive demand. One of the highest-quality small-cap MNCs listed in India.

holdings.name: 3M India
holdings.description: 3M USA holds 75%. Scotch tape, Post-it, safety solutions, healthcare tapes, industrial abrasives. Diversified industrial with thousands of products across segments. Steady royalty model.

holdings.name: Colgate-Palmolive India
holdings.description: Oral care market leader with Colgate brand owning 52% market share in India. Palmolive personal care growing. US parent holds 51%. Resilient consumer staple with pricing power and rural distribution.

holdings.name: Abbott India
holdings.description: Abbott USA holds 75%. Prescription pharma (thyroid, women's health, metabolic), OTC (Duphalac, Digene), and branded formulations. One of the most profitable pharma companies per employee in India.

index.name: Nifty SmallCap 250 Index
index.description: Tracks the Nifty SmallCap 250 index — stocks ranked 251-500 by market cap. Higher risk, higher potential returns over long horizons. Best suited for SIP investors with 7+ year horizon. Captures India's emerging leaders before they become mid or large cap.

holdings.name: Karur Vysya Bank
holdings.description: Tamil Nadu-based private bank with strong South India presence. Vehicle finance, SME, and retail loans. Consistent dividend payer with improving asset quality and expanding digital banking.

holdings.name: KPR Mill
holdings.description: Vertically integrated textile company. Yarn, fabric, garments (FASO brand). Captive power via wind and solar. Ethanol plant from damaged sugarcane. One of the most efficient textile exporters.

holdings.name: Galaxy Surfactants
holdings.description: Specialty surfactants and performance chemicals for FMCG companies globally. Key supplier to HUL, Nestle, P&G. Export revenues >55%. Fatty acid derivatives command premium margins.

holdings.name: Blue Star Ltd
holdings.description: India's second-largest air conditioner brand after Voltas. Commercial refrigeration and HVAC systems for IT parks, malls, and hospitals. Strong after-sales service network.

holdings.name: Chalet Hotels
holdings.description: Upscale hotel developer and operator. Properties co-located with commercial offices in Bengaluru, Mumbai, Hyderabad. Marriott franchised properties. Asset-light management contracts added post-COVID.

holdings.name: CAMS (CAMS Services)
holdings.description: Computer Age Management Services — India's largest mutual fund RTA with 68% market share. Processes 70% of all MF transactions. Insurance repository and KYC businesses diversify revenue.

holdings.name: Sudarshan Chemical
holdings.description: India's largest pigment manufacturer. Exports to over 80 countries. High-performance pigments for automotive coatings, printing inks, and plastics. R&D investments for effect pigments growing.

holdings.name: Birlasoft
holdings.description: Mid-size IT services company spun out of KPIT after merger. ERP (SAP, Oracle), cloud, and digital transformation for manufacturing, BFSI, and life sciences verticals globally.

holdings.name: Mtar Technologies
holdings.description: Precision manufacturing for nuclear, space, and defence sectors. ISRO and DRDO supplier. Fuel cells for defence UAVs. Clean energy components for proton exchange membrane fuel cells.

holdings.name: Shyam Metalics
holdings.description: Integrated metals company: sponge iron, billets, wire rods, ferro alloys. Captive power plants lower opex. Eastern India concentration with proximity to coal and iron ore mines in Jharkhand.

index.name: Nifty 200 Momentum 30 Index
index.description: Selects 30 highest-momentum stocks from Nifty 200 based on 12-month minus 1-month price return, adjusted for volatility. Momentum as a factor has delivered alpha across global markets. Concentrated 30-stock portfolio with higher tracking error vs broad market indices.

holdings.name: Zomato
holdings.description: Consistent positive momentum driven by profitability milestone. Blinkit quick-commerce expansion driving re-rating. Institutional ownership increasing. Strong 12-month price return qualifies for momentum index.

holdings.name: Trent Ltd
holdings.description: Zudio stores' rapid expansion driving exceptional revenue and earnings growth. Consecutive quarterly earnings beats. Strong price momentum with consistent upward earnings revisions.

holdings.name: BSE Ltd
holdings.description: India's oldest stock exchange, beneficiary of retail investor participation boom. Derivatives volumes surging. GIFT City exchange operations growing. Revenue diversification into insurance and other verticals.

holdings.name: Indian Hotels
holdings.description: Tata Group's hotel company. Taj brand globally recognized. Leisure travel boom post-COVID. New hotel openings and Qmin food delivery brand. Strong forward booking visibility.

holdings.name: Torrent Power
holdings.description: Regulated and merchant power utility. Distribution franchise in Ahmedabad, Surat, Agra. Renewable capacity addition. Power sector re-rating on India's energy transition theme.

holdings.name: CESC Ltd
holdings.description: Kolkata's dominant power utility. Regulated ROE provides stable base. Surya Vidyut renewable subsidiary growing. Spencer's Retail stake provides consumer exposure.

holdings.name: IRCTC
holdings.description: Indian Railways' exclusive catering, tourism, and ticketing arm. Internet ticketing monopoly generates massive cash flows. Train travel boom post-COVID. Convenience fee regime restored to full level.

holdings.name: PB Fintech (Policybazaar)
holdings.description: India's largest insurance aggregator and credit marketplace. Policybazaar dominates online term and health insurance. Paisabazaar is India's largest credit marketplace. Path to profitability ahead of schedule.

holdings.name: Persistent Systems
holdings.description: Mid-cap IT services with strong generative AI and digital engineering positioning. Revenue growth consistently above tier-1 peers. US healthcare, BFSI, and hitech verticals. One of the best performing IT stocks in recent years.

holdings.name: Dixon Technologies
