holdings.description: India's largest electronics manufacturer. PLI scheme beneficiary. Assembles smartphones (Samsung, Motorola, Nokia), LED TVs, washing machines. Expanding into set-top boxes and laptops.

name: Mirae Asset Nifty India Consumption ETF

index.name: Nifty India Consumption Index
index.description: Tracks India's domestic consumption theme — FMCG, auto, retail, telecom, travel, media. India's rising middle class, premiumisation trend, and population of 1.4 billion make consumption a generational theme. Urban and rural consumption patterns converging.

holdings.name: Hindustan Unilever
holdings.description: Largest FMCG company. Surf Excel, Dove, Pepsodent, Magnum. Rural distribution of 9M+ outlets. Premium beauty brands (Lakmé, TRESemmé) growing fast. Consistent 90%+ dividend payout ratio.

holdings.name: ITC Ltd
holdings.description: Cigarettes fund FMCG (Aashirvaad, Sunfeast, Bingo) which is India's fastest-growing FMCG. Hotels, agri-business, paper. Hotels demerger unlocking value. ESG improvement with reduction in cigarette dependence.

holdings.name: Maruti Suzuki
holdings.description: ~42% passenger vehicle market share. CNG vehicle leadership. Baleno, Swift, Ertiga top sellers. New SUV launches (Grand Vitara, Fronx, Jimny) targeting premium buyers. Export growth to 100+ countries.

holdings.name: Titan Company
holdings.description: Tata Group's consumer lifestyle powerhouse. Tanishq jewellery (70% revenue), Fastrack/Titan watches, Taneira sarees, Mia gold jewellery. Expanding into eyewear (Titan Eye+) and premium accessories.

holdings.name: Asian Paints
holdings.description: India's dominant decorative paint company with 50%+ market share. Premium Royale and Signature brands. Services (Home Decor, Beautiful Homes) differentiating beyond paint. 26 manufacturing plants.

holdings.name: Avenue Supermarts (DMART)
holdings.description: India's most efficient value retailer. EDLP strategy. 300+ stores in Maharashtra, Gujarat, Andhra. Store economics with 18% ROCE. DMart Ready online for kirana-like convenience.

holdings.name: Trent Ltd
holdings.description: Zudio's ultra-value fashion model disrupting apparel retail. Westside premium stores. Zara and M&S partnerships for luxury fashion. Store count growing 30%+ annually.

holdings.name: Jubilant FoodWorks
holdings.description: Domino's Pizza master franchisee for India, Bangladesh, Nepal, Sri Lanka. Popeyes and Hong's Kitchen new brand launches. 1,900+ Domino's stores. Digital ordering 75%+ of revenues.

holdings.name: Varun Beverages
holdings.description: Pepsi bottler for India and international markets. Sting energy drink disrupting market. Sub-Saharan Africa expansion. Strong revenue growth 20%+ driven by energy drinks and rural distribution push.

holdings.name: Bata India
holdings.description: India's largest footwear company by outlets. Hush Puppies, Comfit, Marie Claire licensed brands. Power brand for affordable schools and workwear. Sneaker collaboration products targeting GenZ.

index.name: Nifty 50 Equal Weight Index
index.description: Assigns equal 2% weight to all 50 Nifty stocks regardless of market cap. Reduces concentration risk from HDFC Bank, Reliance, and ICICI Bank dominating market-cap indices. Historically outperforms cap-weighted Nifty in mid-cycle recoveries and small-cap rallies. Ideal for investors believing in broader market participation.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: Equal weight means Reliance gets 2% vs 10%+ in cap-weighted Nifty. Reduces single-stock concentration. Retail, Jio, and O2C segments driving diversified revenue.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Same 2% as all others vs 13%+ in cap-weighted Nifty. Equal weight reduces excessive banking sector concentration.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Equal 2% weight. Reduces IT sector dominance of market-cap index. Cobalt AI platform and generative AI services driving new revenue streams.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Equal 2% gives same importance as smaller Nifty constituents. Transformation under CEO Bakhshi continues to deliver.

holdings.name: TCS
holdings.description: India's largest IT exporter. Equal weighting reduces exposure to mega-cap IT. AI and cloud services growing share of revenues.

holdings.name: Bajaj Finance
holdings.description: India's largest NBFC. Equal weight elevates Bajaj Finance to same prominence as banking giants. Consumer lending and EMI financing.

holdings.name: Larsen & Toubro
holdings.description: Equal weight elevates L&T vs cap-weighted index. Order book ₹4.7 trillion across infrastructure, defence, and technology segments.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical
holdings.description: Equal weight gives pharma same prominence as banks. US specialty products (Ilumya, Cequa, Winlevi) diversifying beyond Indian formulations.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Conservative underwriting culture. Equal weight philosophy aligns with fundamental value approach rather than market-cap momentum.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: Post-Citi integration bank with premium credit card franchise. Equal weight treats Axis at same level as larger banking peers.

index.name: Nifty Bharat Bond Index April 2030
index.methodology: Target Maturity — Roll-down AAA PSU bonds
index.description: India's first corporate bond ETF tracking AAA-rated PSU bonds maturing around April 2030. Edelweiss AMC launched Bharat Bond as a landmark fixed-income product. Hold-to-maturity strategy: if held till April 2030, investor earns the yield at purchase (predictable returns). Ultra-low expense ratio of 0.0005% — cheapest debt product in India.

holdings.name: NHAI (Natl Highways Auth)
holdings.description: NHAI AAA-rated bonds maturing 2029-30. Backed by Government of India. Funds National Highway construction programme. Sovereign-equivalent credit quality with slightly higher yield than G-Secs.

holdings.name: REC Ltd (Rural Electrification)
holdings.description: REC Ltd bonds rated AAA by CRISIL and ICRA. Funds rural electrification and renewable energy projects. 54EC capital gains bonds also issued. GoI owns 52.63% — implicit sovereign backing.

holdings.name: PFC (Power Finance Corp)
holdings.description: PFC AAA bonds. Largest infrastructure finance company for power sector. Funds generation, transmission, and distribution projects. GoI owns 55.99%. Strong NPA coverage ratios.

holdings.name: NTPC Ltd
holdings.description: India's largest power utility bonds. AAA-rated. NTPC expanding aggressively into solar (60 GW target by 2032). Green bonds also issued for renewable projects. Extremely safe with PSU backing.

holdings.name: Indian Railway Finance Corp
holdings.description: IRFC AAA bonds fund Indian Railways capital expenditure. IRFC leases rolling stock and infrastructure to Indian Railways which repays from budget allocation — zero credit risk model.

holdings.name: HUDCO (Housing & Urban Dev)
holdings.description: HUDCO AAA bonds fund affordable housing and urban infrastructure. PMAY (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana) projects financed. Government of India holds 81.78% stake.

holdings.name: Nabard
holdings.description: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development bonds. AAA-rated. Refinances cooperative and rural banks for agricultural lending. GoI + RBI hold 100% — zero default risk.

holdings.name: SIDBI
holdings.description: Small Industries Development Bank of India bonds. AAA-rated. Provides refinancing to banks and NBFCs lending to MSMEs. Government-owned development financial institution.

holdings.name: Exim Bank
holdings.description: Export-Import Bank of India bonds. AAA-rated. Funds India's export credit and project exports. Strategic importance in India's trade finance architecture. 100% GoI-owned.

holdings.name: Gas Authority (GAIL)
holdings.description: GAIL (India) Ltd bonds. AAA-rated. India's largest natural gas transmission and distribution company. Petrochemicals and city gas distribution. Navratna PSU under MoPNG.

index.name: Nifty Bharat Bond Index April 2033
index.methodology: Target Maturity — Roll-down AAA PSU bonds
index.description: Second series of Bharat Bond ETF targeting April 2033 maturity. Longer duration (7+ years) means higher yield and slightly more interest rate sensitivity than the 2030 series. Same ultra-safe AAA PSU bond universe. Ideal for investors targeting a specific financial goal around 2033 — retirement, child's education, or property purchase.

holdings.name: NHAI (Natl Highways Auth)
holdings.description: Longer-dated NHAI bonds maturing 2032-33. NHAI is executing the world's second-largest highway construction programme. Bharatmala Phase-I of 34,800 km. Zero sovereign default risk.

holdings.name: REC Ltd
holdings.description: REC longer-dated bonds at slightly higher yield than shorter series. Huge opportunity in India's 500 GW renewable energy target by 2030 — REC will fund a large portion of this capex.

holdings.name: PFC (Power Finance Corp)
holdings.description: PFC 2033-maturity bonds. GoI's revised estimates project India needing $500bn in power sector investment by 2030 — PFC and REC are primary funding channels. AAA stable outlook.

holdings.name: NTPC Ltd
holdings.description: NTPC's green bond series included. Company targeting 60 GW renewable capacity. Green bonds raise cheaper capital for solar/wind projects. S&P and Moody's rated investment grade.

holdings.name: Indian Railway Finance Corp
holdings.description: IRFC 2033-maturity bonds. Indian Railways ₹2.6 trillion annual capex plan fully funded through IRFC. Passenger and freight revenue supports repayment. Effectively sovereign-guaranteed.

holdings.name: HUDCO
holdings.description: HUDCO 2033 bonds fund Smart City projects, metro rail, affordable housing. Urban India investment pipeline is massive with 100 smart cities programme. GoI backing ensures AAA rating.

holdings.name: Nabard
holdings.description: NABARD longer-dated bonds. India's farm credit growth target: ₹20 lakh crore for FY24. NABARD refinancing supports Kisan Credit Cards, microfinance, and rural SHGs.

holdings.name: SIDBI
holdings.description: SIDBI 2033 bonds. India has 63 million MSMEs employing 110 million people. SIDBI's refinance operations are critical to this sector's formal credit access. GoI-owned DFI.

holdings.name: Exim Bank
holdings.description: Exim Bank 2033 bonds. India targeting $1 trillion exports by FY28 — Exim Bank credit lines to African and ASEAN nations are strategic geopolitical instruments.

holdings.name: NHB (Natl Housing Bank)
holdings.description: National Housing Bank bonds. NHB regulates all housing finance companies in India. Refinances HFCs (HDFC, LIC Housing) at concessional rates for affordable housing. RBI subsidiary.

name: ICICI Prudential Nifty 10yr Benchmark G-Sec ETF

index.name: Nifty 10yr Benchmark G-Sec Index
index.description: Tracks the current on-the-run 10-year Indian Government Security (benchmark G-Sec). Pure sovereign credit — zero default risk. Ideal for interest rate plays: buy when rates are expected to fall, as G-Sec prices rise when yields fall. Included in JP Morgan GBI-EM index (June 2024) — massive global demand catalyst. RBI's benchmark rate instrument.

holdings.name: GoI 7.10% 2034
holdings.description: Current on-the-run 10-year Government of India Security. Coupon 7.10%, maturing 2034. Included in JP Morgan GBI-EM Global Diversified index since June 2024 — triggers ~$25 billion in passive foreign inflows. RBI Open Market Operations use this as primary instrument. Zero credit risk, backed by sovereign guarantee.

name: Nippon India ETF Nifty 5yr Benchmark G-Sec

index.name: Nifty 5yr Benchmark G-Sec Index
index.description: Tracks the on-the-run 5-year Government of India Security. Shorter duration than the 10yr ETF — less interest rate sensitivity. Suitable for investors wanting sovereign safety with moderate duration. Yield slightly below 10yr providing the normal upward-sloping yield curve exposure.

holdings.name: GoI 7.02% 2029
holdings.description: Current on-the-run 5-year Government of India Security. Coupon 7.02%, maturing 2029. Shorter duration means less price volatility for same change in yield. Preferred by conservative investors wanting G-Sec exposure without duration risk. Actively traded on NDS-OM platform by banks and PDs.

name: Nippon India ETF Nifty SDL Apr 2026

index.name: Nifty SDL Apr 2026 Index
index.description: Tracks a basket of State Development Loans (SDLs) — bonds issued by individual state governments — maturing around April 2026. SDLs carry quasi-sovereign credit with ~25-40 bps spread over Central G-Secs. Backed by state revenue and transfers from Centre. Higher yield than G-Secs with near-sovereign safety. Target maturity structure gives predictable returns if held to maturity.

holdings.name: Maharashtra SDL 2026
holdings.description: Maharashtra SDLs — India's largest state economy. Mumbai financial hub generates massive GST and stamp duty revenues. Strong fiscal position. SDL yield ~25 bps above central G-Sec.

holdings.name: Tamil Nadu SDL 2026
holdings.description: Tamil Nadu SDLs. Manufacturing hub — auto, pharma, electronics. Strong revenue base from GST, electricity tariffs. Chennai-Bengaluru-Coimbatore industrial corridor driving growth.

holdings.name: Karnataka SDL 2026
holdings.description: Karnataka SDLs. Bengaluru's IT sector makes Karnataka a high-GST state. Stamp duty from booming real estate adds to revenue. Well-managed fiscal position relative to peers.

holdings.name: Gujarat SDL 2026
holdings.description: Gujarat SDLs. India's most industrialised state. GIFT City, petrochemicals (Jamnagar), pharmaceuticals, textiles. Low fiscal deficit ratio. Premium borrowing terms vs other states.

holdings.name: Uttar Pradesh SDL 2026
holdings.description: UP SDLs. India's most populous state and largest economy by GSDP in Hindi belt. Expressway infrastructure, defence corridor, data centers. Improving fiscal management under current government.

holdings.name: Rajasthan SDL 2026
holdings.description: Rajasthan SDLs. Renewable energy powerhouse — largest wind and solar installed capacity. Mining royalties from oil, lead, zinc. Higher spread due to slightly elevated fiscal deficit.

holdings.name: Andhra Pradesh SDL 2026
holdings.description: AP SDLs. Port-led industrialisation (Visakhapatnam, Kakinada). Aquaculture and agriculture exports. New capital Amravati construction restarted. Higher yield due to fiscal stress history.

holdings.name: Telangana SDL 2026
holdings.description: Telangana SDLs. Hyderabad pharma city, IT and biotech cluster. Youngest state with growing tax base. Infrastructure investment in ORR, Metro Phase-2, and Data Center parks driving borrowing.

holdings.name: Madhya Pradesh SDL 2026
holdings.description: MP SDLs. Agricultural surplus state — wheat, soybean production. Tourism revenue from Khajuraho, Kanha National Park. Industrial corridors and Smart Cities investment increasing.

holdings.name: West Bengal SDL 2026
holdings.description: West Bengal SDLs. Kolkata port, jute, tea, and engineering industries. Highest SDL spread among major states due to elevated debt-to-GSDP. Compensated by higher yield for risk-tolerant investors.
